name: Chrysaor
shortDescription: Chrysaor, whose name means 'he of the golden sword,' was a giant in Greek mythology, born from the severed neck of Medusa. He is often associated with his brother Pegasus.
description: Chrysaor was a giant in Greek mythology, born from the severed neck of the Gorgon Medusa when Perseus beheaded her. His father was Poseidon. He sprang forth, fully grown, alongside his brother Pegasus. While Pegasus is far more famous, Chrysaor is an important, if lesser-known, figure. He is typically depicted as a warrior, often associated with a golden sword, which gives him his name. Chrysaor married Callirrhoe, daughter of Oceanus, and together they had Geryon, a monstrous giant with three bodies joined at the waist. Geryon was eventually slain by Heracles as one of his twelve labors, further linking Chrysaor to the most famous of Greek heroes, albeit indirectly through his offspring. He is not typically involved in complex narratives beyond his birth and parentage, but his role as the father of Geryon places him firmly within the lineage of monstrous figures that populate Greek myths. His existence highlights the chaotic and often monstrous offspring that could arise from the unions of gods and mortals or gods and monsters.
appearance: Depicted as a fully grown warrior, often associated with a golden sword.
